title: Electronic word-of-mouth via consumer-opinion platforms: What motivates consumers to articulate themselves on the Internet?
key_takeaway: Concern for other consumers, social interaction, economic incentives, and self-enhancement are major drivers of review writing.

title: What Motivates Consumers to Write Online Travel Reviews?
key_takeaway: Travel review writers are mainly motivated by helping providers, helping other consumers, enjoyment, and positive self-enhancement.

title: Online travel review posting intentions: a social exchange theory perspective
key_takeaway: Altruism, reputation, economic rewards, and venting increase intent, while cognitive and executional costs reduce it.

title: What motivates posting online travel reviews? Integrating gratifications with technological acceptance factors
key_takeaway: Altruism, reciprocity, habit, hedonic motivation, and ease of use shape posting behavior.

title: Understanding Users' Urge to Post Online Reviews: A Study Based on Existence, Relatedness, and Growth Theory
key_takeaway: Intrinsic motives such as helping others and supporting the website strongly affect the urge to post reviews.

title: Motivation crowding in online product reviewing: A qualitative study of amazon reviewers
focus: Interaction of intrinsic and extrinsic motives
key_takeaway: Extrinsic rewards can either reinforce or crowd out intrinsic motivation.

title: Identifying Factors Motivating Users to Post Reviews on Online Travel Review Platforms: A Factor Analysis Study
key_takeaway: Peer support, social capital, and personal reasoning remain important drivers of travel review posting.

title: Altruism in eWOM: Propensity to Write Reviews on Hotel Experience
venue_or_source: Journal of Theoretical and Applied Electronic Commerce Research
key_takeaway: Among hotel customers, altruism is the strongest underlying motivation to write reviews.

title: Exploring the antecedents for hospitality reviewers' trustworthiness and its impact on business patronage
key_takeaway: Reviewer trustworthiness is shaped by both reviewer profile information and qualitative text features.

title: Examining the effect of reviewer expertise and personality on reviewer satisfaction: An empirical study of TripAdvisor
key_takeaway: Reviewer expertise and personality significantly shape review ratings and text sentiment.

title: Hospitality and Tourism Online Review Research: A Systematic Analysis and Heuristic-Systematic Model
focus: Meta review of hospitality online reviews
key_takeaway: Source, review, context, and receiver cues jointly shape online review creation and interpretation.

title: Conspicuous Reviewing: Affiliation with High-status Organizations as a Motivation for Writing Online Reviews
key_takeaway: Some reviewers write to make their consumption behavior publicly visible and status-relevant.

title: TripAdvisor Launches Guide for Writing Helpful Reviews Based on Survey of More Than 100,000
key_takeaway: Travelers prefer reviews that are factual, detailed, recent, contextual, and balanced.

title: Research: What makes a helpful review
key_takeaway: Lack of detail reduces helpfulness; context and personality help.

title: Moderating effects of rating on text and helpfulness in online hotel reviews: an analytical approach
key_takeaway: Text and star ratings interact in shaping review helpfulness.

title: Assessing the helpfulness of online hotel reviews: A classification-based approach
key_takeaway: Review quality, sentiment, and reviewer characteristics jointly predict helpfulness.

title: The Determinants of Helpful Hotel Reviews: A Social Influence Perspective
key_takeaway: Review length, ratings, and social influence factors affect hotel review helpfulness.

title: What makes an online consumer review helpful? A diagnosticity-adoption framework to explain informational and normative influences in e-WOM
venue_or_source: Journal of the Academy of Marketing Science
key_takeaway: Message cues and reviewer cues improve review diagnosticity and adoption.

title: Navigating by the stars: Investigating the actual and perceived validity of online user ratings
venue_or_source: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes
key_takeaway: Star ratings alone can be misleading, which increases the value of richer textual reviews.

title: Rant or rave: variation over time in the language of online reviews
key_takeaway: Review length and submission conditions affect helpfulness; mobile-written reviews tend to be shorter and less helpful.
